title: gilia pungens subsp. hallii
intro: Gilia pungens subsp. hallii, also known as Hall's gilia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Polemoniaceae family. It is primarily found in the western United States, particularly in the mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Hall's gilia include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems, and it can be prepared into various herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
